response.text:
## REFLECTION

Household r4c3 is in a precarious energy position: with only 1.67 kWh of 3 kWh capacity (55.7% SoC) and a 1.00 kW load, the battery will be depleted in approximately 1.67 hours if no external supply arrives. The memory log reveals a concerning pattern—three geographic neighbors (r3c3, r4c2, r4c4) have been repeatedly requesting energy over the past hour, and r4c3 has been accepting those requests despite its own declining SoC trajectory (1.67 → 1.51 → 1.34 → 1.17 → 1.00 kWh). This suggests r4c3 has been operating under a balanced or generous sharing policy that prioritizes grid equity, but this altruism is now threatening its own survival. With no trust circles established and operating in island mode, r4c3 has no preferential relationships and no guarantee of reciprocal support. The repeated acceptance of requests from peers whose SoC fractions (0.35–0.49) are not dramatically lower than r4c3's own current state (0.56) indicates a misalignment between policy intent and operational reality. Immediate policy correction is essential to preserve minimum operational capacity.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.40
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.1
recipient_priority:
  - circle: geographic
    weight: 0.3
recipient_distrusted_peers:
  - r3c3
  - r4c2
  - r4c4
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "SoC critically low after repeated energy sharing. Peers have not reciprocated. Switching to conservative mode to preserve minimum operational margin. Will only share if own SoC remains above 40% and only at 0.1 kW/tick rate."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
